breadcrumbs.id: windows security monitoring/windows threat detection 1/initial access via usb
breadcrumbs.title: Initial Access via USB

overview: Although some may believe that days of infected USB flash drives are long gone and cloud services have replaced them completely, threat actors will disagree, as proven by Camaro Dragon or Raspberry Robin attacks. Moreover, Initial Access via an infected USB bypasses firewalls, much like phishing, and can start the attack chain even without Internet access an

study.overview: Although some may believe that days of infected USB flash drives are long gone and cloud services have replaced them completely, threat actors will disagree, as proven by Camaro Dragon or Raspberry Robin attacks. Moreover, Initial Access via an infected USB bypasses firewalls, much like phishing, and can start the attack chain even without Internet access an
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
